title: Tokyo Hibachi Catering
name: Christopher Tomesko
text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5/5 — Absolutely Outstanding Experience!

Tokyo Hibachi Catering was phenomenal, and Kevin was truly unbelievable! From start to finish, the entire experience exceeded expectations. Kevin brought incredible energy, skill, and showmanship to the event — everyone was entertained and amazed. The food was fresh, perfectly cooked, and full of flavor — restaurant quality (if not better!).

Kevin went above and beyond to make sure every guest had a great time. He was professional, fun, and made the entire experience unforgettable. It’s rare to find someone who can deliver top-tier food and a fantastic performance like that.

I can’t recommend Tokyo Hibachi Catering — and especially Kevin — highly enough. If you’re looking to turn your event into something truly special, this is the team to book!

text:
I've never been to a fine dining restaurant before. But, it was my parents' anniversary, so I decided it was worth a shot.

The staff custom made anniversary cards and provided us with complimentary desserts as well. Everyone was very nice and accommodating, really warm atmosphere coupled with the view of NYC 60 stories up.

The food too - although they weren't the largest portions, everything was delicious. Some dishes had a bit too much sauce and saltiness for my liking, but you cannot deny the quality and craft that goes into it.

While it is pricey, around $140 for the four course set, I would still recommend it for special occasions if you want to celebrate or ball out for once.

title: Eddie's Pizza
name: Jodie Simpson
text:
I been going to Eddie's since I was a little boy... no joke! My parents use to own a shop nearby and it was great, don't let this place fool you, looks like some typical pizza joint - nah, this place has some amazing history and is a bar pie place and has some amazing food. The chicken dishes are amazing, you can get cherry peppers on your bar pie which make it very amazing. Every  waiter and waitress here is super nice and kind. They have a jukebox, this place feels like a gangster hangout and it is amazing. Awesome old movie posters on the walls, this place is great! It is very nostalgic for me at 25 years old but this place also rubs my heart that this is what old NY was like, this place gives off that vibe of a NY very long gone... We can cheerish and enjoy it here.

The food is great, the vibe is great, everyone is great. I am unsure if the man with grey hair works here still but he was amazing also such a funny kind guy. TRY THIS PLACE! Have a coors and relax and feel like you're one of the gangsters who would import it illegally to the East Coast back in the day :)

name: Andy
text:
I don’t usually leave bad reviews, but this place really let me down. The pizza was a disaster — extremely floppy, the sauce tasted like it came straight out of a cheap can with zero flavor, and the dough itself tasted cheap and low quality. I also tried the lasagna and for $18 I was given a tiny sliver that was nowhere near worth the price. The garlic knots were undercooked and doughy in the middle, which just made things worse.

The staff themselves were fine — friendly and trying their best — but the storefront has a literal hole and has gone downhill so badly. Unfortunately, the food is just terrible and not worth coming back for.

title: The Landing
name: David Asekoff
text:
Went to a fantastic bar / lounge near MSG / Penn Station / Herald's Square today. It's called the Landing in 1 Penn Plaza, Manhattan, NYC.

Second floor. Live music / DJ, no cover, and fantastic drinks and service. Cocktails run about $18 a pop. Upscale. Huge lounge area with big screens frequently with sports content. Comfortable seating and even areas to sit down with a laptop and take calls / meetings.

Great area to chill while waiting for an event or train. Meet up place for friends or business.

Bathrooms are unisex and super clean.

I'll take photos next time. It's a hidden gem. Thank me later folks.

Food and drinks were fine. The experience, however, was ruined, by the waiter shaming us into providing an 18% tip, instead of a 20% tip. (both were options displayed on the card reader, mind you)

After paying the bill, he stared at us and then said:
"Is this your first time here?"
"Yes"
"Are you residents in the building?"
"No"
He then condescendingly said "Ah, that makes sense".

Unprofessional and petty behaviour.

title: The Landing
name: EE Grimshaw
text:
***3 1/2 stars***

I've been to The Landing a few times now, primarily for drinks after work and once for a sit-down lunch. I've never had a bad experience here, but there's also nothing about this place that really pops or blows me away. Not necessarily a negative, and arguably it's good enough.

The best part about The Landing is the location/convenience. I work in the building at Penn1, so this is literally just downstairs - if you're an office tenant, you can just take the elevator to the second floor "social stairs" area and walk over to it without even navigating the lobby traffic. I get the impression most of the clientele here is in the same boat as I am, which makes sense - if you're just walking by on the street, the signage isn't particularly prominent and your eyes are probably going to get drawn to something else anyway. It's usually packed here after work (probably other people in the building with the same idea), but they let you hang out on the social stairs outside of the restaurant and utilize their services from there. Their staff just walks around taking and providing orders. Very cool, and a smart way on their end to maximize business.

I was most recently here this past Thursday evening for my department happy hour (everything tastes better on the company card). Despite an attempt around 4:30pm to grab a table, nothing was available so off to the steps we went, happy hour menu in hand. I ordered an Old Fashioned with Angel's Envy (my favorite bourbon, which thankfully has always been here when I've asked for it), and requested the Crispy Rice app, which is their version of tuna tartare with copious amounts of avocado and a few pieces of crispy rice to dip or eat off of. The Old Fashioneds are decent here and pretty straight-forward. There's an argument that for me all Old Fashioneds are graded on a curve if they have the Angel's Envy, but it was well made nonetheless. Maybe a TOUCH heavy on the bourbon but I'm nitpicking. The Crispy Rice is always a good time as well - I think they could benefit from including twice as many pieces of the crispy rice, which disappears almost instantly, leaving a large pile of the tuna tartare to play around with, but it is what it is. The fish and avocado are fresh, and that to me is the basic requirement of the dish.

The service is usually pretty decent here. By definition, I think it's almost impossible to maintain a truly excellent level of hospitality during the time of the day/night when your staff has to be stretched very thin across half the second floor of the building basically, but it could be worse. A few times we were all looking around for a waitress to order additional drinks/apps but it was never an egregious amount of time and arguably inevitable with the setup. We had a few people assisting us, and I appreciated the fact that one of the waitresses (after receiving an app order technically after happy hour ended) told us the price was no longer applicable but that she didn't let us know ahead of time, so she was going to try to ensure we still got the deal. Classy and a pretty easy win for them.

The Landing really benefits from the location in two ways - one is the aforementioned convenience factor if you're already in the building, but two is that you can avoid going outside and dealing with arguably the worst neighborhood in NYC. If that's not a win, I don't know what is.

Despite it being 8pm on a Saturday night, the team were incredibly accommodating and managed to find us a spot in the bar area after a short wait, which was made much more pleasant with a couple of cocktails. Considering how busy it was, this was no small feat and very much appreciated.

We ordered the duck wings, fries and the lobster roll, all of which were excellent. The wine was also superb – from memory, a Pinot Noir that paired perfectly with the food.

The views from the restaurant are quite honestly some of the best in the world, and combined with the quality of the food, it made for a fantastic experience. I’d love to return and try the main menu next time – I’ll just make sure I book the right day.

title: Manhatta
name: Prachi Patke
text:
We went to Manhattan for our anniversary lunch and left feeling really disappointed by the food, despite some genuine positives.

To start with the good: the service staff was excellent—attentive, warm, and professional throughout. The restaurant was clearly prepared for our anniversary; we had a beautiful table and a fantastic view, which made the setting feel special.

Unfortunately, the dining experience fell apart once it came to the food and drinks.

When I asked for a simple martini with a twist or flavor, the bartender said it wasn’t possible, which was surprising given that these are standard customizations. We were told to choose only from the cocktail menu, which felt unnecessarily restrictive.

The bigger issue was the food. We had informed the restaurant well in advance that one of the guests was vegetarian, yet the preset three-course menu had no vegetarian entrée at all. The only option offered was a “custom” pasta, which turned out to be plain al dente pasta with basic tomato ragu—no vegetables, no garnish, no effort. I even asked while ordering if vegetables could be added and was told no. Eating unadorned pasta with tomato sauce on your wedding anniversary is… not great.

My husband’s burger was also below average—nothing memorable and not what you’d expect at this price point. Desserts were just okay; the sorbet, in particular, was quite bad.

Overall, the views and service were the only saving grace. For a restaurant that presents itself as upscale and special-occasion worthy, the food quality and lack of flexibility—especially around dietary needs that were communicated ahead of time—were deeply disappointing. Sadly, not a place we’d return to or recommend for a celebratory meal.

title: Thai Villa
name: Derek Bolt
text:
Thai Villa delivers one of those meals that makes you slow down—because every dish feels intentional, balanced, and genuinely cared for. The space is gorgeous and transportive, with a warm, intimate glow that makes the whole night feel like an occasion without trying too hard.

What really stands out is the precision of the flavors: bright herbs, deep aromatics, clean heat, and that rare ability to be bold without getting heavy. Every bite tastes layered—sweet, sour, salty, spicy—built in harmony instead of competing for attention. Even the classics feel elevated, with textures that stay crisp where they should and richness that never turns dull.

Service matches the food: polished, kind, and confident, with pacing that lets you enjoy the meal instead of rushing you through it. Thai Villa isn’t just “good Thai”—it’s the kind of place you remember, crave again a week later, and recommend immediately.

Continue crushing it please.

text:
Thai Villa is hands down our favorite Thai restaurant in the city. We’ve been here multiple times, and it never disappoints. The food is consistently amazing — full of flavor, beautifully presented, and clearly made with care.

A special thank you to our server, Andy, who provided outstanding service. She was attentive, friendly, and made sure everything was perfect throughout our meal.

If you’re looking for authentic Thai cuisine with exceptional service, Thai Villa is the place to go. We’ll definitely be back again soon!

title: Thai Villa
name: Dhruvi Patel
text:
I had an amazing experience at this Thai restaurant! The Pad Kee Mao (drunken noodles) was hands down the best I’ve ever had — perfectly balanced flavors, just the right level of spice, and fresh ingredients that really stood out. Every bite was delicious and satisfying.
What made the experience even better was our server, GAM V. She was incredibly friendly, attentive, and genuinely helpful throughout our visit. She made sure we had everything we needed and gave great recommendations. Her warm and welcoming attitude truly elevated our dining experience.
If you’re looking for authentic Thai food with fantastic service, this place is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be coming back!

title: Thai Villa
name: Coral Wu
text:
I can confidently say this is one of the best Thai restaurants I’ve ever had. From the moment we walked in, everything felt special. The ambiance is absolutely beautiful — elegant, warm, and thoughtfully designed, making it perfect for a date night or a memorable dinner with friends.

The food was outstanding from start to finish. Every dish was beautifully presented and packed with authentic, well-balanced flavors. You can really tell the kitchen puts care and attention into every plate.

Our server, James, was incredibly attentive — friendly, professional, and always checking in at just the right moments. A big thank you to Riz as well for recommending so many delicious dishes. We tried several items and loved them all, but our favorite was definitely the duck chuchee — perfectly cooked with a rich, flavorful sauce. The Royal Pad Thai is also a must-try dish.

We will definitely be coming back again soon. And thank you again, Riz, for the amazing drink recommendation — it was fantastic, and I absolutely love the name: “Kiss All the Time” :)

In addition, while my allergy of sesame and cashews was accounted for in our Spicy Chicken Wings and Moo-obb Nam Puang  — although for our dessert: Khao Neaw Phuak, it still contained sesame on it, thankfully my girlfriend noticed it before I could. Overall, our time at Thai Villa may not have been ideal, but it was still worth it for the food and atmosphere alone. A respectable 3/5, I see myself coming back possibly.

Our mistake was not saying something at the time.

I had the steak frites and my wife had the duck. Both dishes were excellent, as well as the escargot appetizer. The fact that the captain came by to see how we were doing and also the general manager, Erica as well, speaks volumes about not only the food and ambience, but also the service and attention to detail. We will absolutely be coming again for dinner and also the brunch, which I’m told is a wonderful experience as well.

text: Came on Valentine’s Day and expected the usual rush and busy atmosphere. There was a busy atmosphere but what I did not expect was to be completely forgotten about. My bf and I sat down at 9:15 and ordered apps right away. Then we had to ask 3 diff waiters for our waiter to order dinner. Once we did we waited over an hour without the main course. I had to go up to the host and ask what time the kitchen closed bc I knew our waiter forgot to send our food and i did not want to leave without our food. Then another waiter had to grab our waiter and remind him we were his table. He then rushed to the kitchen and they made our food so unbelievably mid. My bf steak was still mooing and my salmon had absolutely no garnish or presentation. We felt with it and when it came time for the bill we honestly thought somthing would be comped bc we ordered our food at 9:45 and got dinner at 11:15. Nothing was of course and we paid the $200 lousy tap and tipped. Would not recommend this place to anyone ever. Was horrible experience and poor customer service. Throughout the dinner if we wanted a drink or more bread we had to ask 3 times minimum. So many great places in NYC don’t bother here.

title: Boucherie Union Square
name: Elizabeth D'Angelo
text: Most restaurants are expensive nowadays but this one I can actually say was worth it. The food, service, and atmosphere were just incredible. The steak was cooked to perfection and the french onion soup was divine. The staff was also so charming and attentive. Would definitely come back for special occasions, dates, or dinners with the family.

title: Boucherie Union Square
name: Holly Hicks
text: We celebrated a birthday lunch at Boucherie Union Square before visiting Anne Frank, The Exhibition at the nearby Center for Jewish History. Our server, Gaudi, provided wonderful service with friendly and pleasant conversation, excellent recommendations, and a special birthday treat for my husband. I had French onion soup, with croutons and Gruyère and the Le Croque Madame with Jambon, Gruyère, egg, and petite salade, both delicious. My husband had the Beef Bourguignon, a Traditional French braised beef, with carrots, mushrooms, bacon, and pommes purée. After these delicious dishes, we enjoyed a special birthday treat as well as a Boucherie book with history, photos, and recipes! Thank you, Boucherie and Gaudi!
